title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: members, contributors, leaders pledge make participation community harassment-free experience everyone, regardless age, body size, visible invisible disability, ethnicity, sex characteristics, gender identity expression, level experience, education, socio-economic status, nationality, personal appearance, race, caste, color, religion, sexual identity orientation. pledge act interact ways contribute open, welcoming, diverse, inclusive, healthy community.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Examples behavior contributes positive environment community include: Demonstrating empathy kindness toward people respectful differing opinions, viewpoints, experiences Giving gracefully accepting constructive feedback Accepting responsibility apologizing affected mistakes, learning experience Focusing best just us individuals, overall community Examples unacceptable behavior include: use sexualized language imagery, sexual attention advances kind Trolling, insulting derogatory comments, personal political attacks Public private harassment Publishing others’ private information, physical email address, without explicit permission conduct reasonably considered inappropriate professional setting

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community leaders responsible clarifying enforcing standards acceptable behavior take appropriate fair corrective action response behavior deem inappropriate, threatening, offensive, harmful. Community leaders right responsibility remove, edit, reject comments, commits, code, wiki edits, issues, contributions aligned Code Conduct, communicate reasons moderation decisions appropriate.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Code Conduct applies within community spaces, also applies individual officially representing community public spaces. Examples representing community include using official e-mail address, posting via official social media account, acting appointed representative online offline event.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Instances abusive, harassing, otherwise unacceptable behavior may reported community leaders responsible enforcement wjakethompson@gmail.com. complaints reviewed investigated promptly fairly. community leaders obligated respect privacy security reporter incident.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community leaders follow Community Impact Guidelines determining consequences action deem violation Code Conduct:

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community Impact: Use inappropriate language behavior deemed unprofessional unwelcome community. Consequence: private, written warning community leaders, providing clarity around nature violation explanation behavior inappropriate. public apology may requested.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community Impact: violation single incident series actions. Consequence: warning consequences continued behavior. interaction people involved, including unsolicited interaction enforcing Code Conduct, specified period time. includes avoiding interactions community spaces well external channels like social media. Violating terms may lead temporary permanent ban.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community Impact: serious violation community standards, including sustained inappropriate behavior. Consequence: temporary ban sort interaction public communication community specified period time. public private interaction people involved, including unsolicited interaction enforcing Code Conduct, allowed period. Violating terms may lead permanent ban.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community Impact: Demonstrating pattern violation community standards, including sustained inappropriate behavior, harassment individual, aggression toward disparagement classes individuals. Consequence: permanent ban sort public interaction within community.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Code Conduct adapted Contributor Covenant, version 2.1, available https://www.contributor-covenant.org/version/2/1/code_of_conduct.html. Community Impact Guidelines inspired [Mozilla’s code conduct enforcement ladder][https://github.com/mozilla/inclusion]. answers common questions code conduct, see FAQ https://www.contributor-covenant.org/faq. Translations available https://www.contributor-covenant.org/translations.

title: Contributing to dcmdata
text: want make bigger change, ’s good idea first file issue make sure someone team agrees ’s needed. ’ve found bug, please file issue illustrates bug minimal reprex (also help write unit test, needed). See tidyverse guide create great issue advice.

title: Contributing to dcmdata
text: Fork package clone onto computer. haven’t done , recommend using usethis::create_from_github("r-dcm/dcmdata", fork = TRUE). Install development dependencies devtools::install_dev_deps(), make sure package passes R CMD check running devtools::check(). R CMD check doesn’t pass cleanly, ’s good idea ask help continuing. Create Git branch pull request (PR). recommend using usethis::pr_init("brief-description--change"). Make changes, commit git, create PR running usethis::pr_push(), following prompts browser. title PR briefly describe change. body PR contain Fixes #issue-number. user-facing changes, add bullet top NEWS.md (.e. just first header). Follow style described https://style.tidyverse.org/news.html.

title: Contributing to dcmdata
text: New code follow tidyverse style guide. can use styler package apply styles, please don’t restyle code nothing PR. use roxygen2, Markdown syntax, documentation. use testthat unit tests. Contributions test cases included easier accept.

title: Data Sets for Diagnostic Classification Modeling
text: dcmdata contains real simulated data sets educational psychological assessment. information data set, see linked reference pages.

title: Data Sets for Diagnostic Classification Modeling
text: ECPE: Assessment data Examination Certificate Proficiency English, described Templin Hoffman (2013). Fraction: fraction subtraction data described Tatsuoka (2002). MCMI: Psychological assessment data Millon Clinical Multiaxial Inventory-III, described Rossi et al. (2010). MDM: short integer multiplication assessment, described MacReady Dayton (1977). PIE: Assessment data Pathways Instructionally Embedded Assessment data, described ATLAS (2025). ROAR-PA: Data Rapid Online Assessment Reading Phonological Awareness, described Gijbels et al. (2024). TIMSS-03: Data 2003 grade 8 Trends International Mathematics Science Study, describe Skaggs et al. (2016). TIMSS-07: Data 2007 grade 4 Trends International Mathematics Science Study, describe Park et al. (2011).

title: Data Sets for Diagnostic Classification Modeling
text: DTMR: data set based Diagnostic Teachers’ Multiplicative Reasoning assessment, described Bradshaw et al. (2014).

title: Data Sets for Diagnostic Classification Modeling
text: Contributions welcome. ensure smooth process, please review Contributing Guide. Please note dcmdata project released Contributor Code Conduct. contributing project, agree abide terms.

Based attributes required possessed, use dtmr_true_items calculate log odds respondent providing correct response item. dtmr_true_items contains estimated item parameters reported Table 1 Bradshaw et al. (2014). tibble 27 rows 7 columns. item: Item identifier, corresponds 1-22 dtmr_data. intercept: LCDM intercept parameter item. referent_units: LCDM main effect parameter items measuring referent units attribute. partitioning_iterating: LCDM main effect parameter items measuring partitioning iterating attribute. appropriateness: LCDM main effect parameter items measuring appropriateness attribute. multiplicative_comparison: LCDM main effect parameter items measuring multiplicative comparisons attribute. referent_units__partitioning_iterating: LCDM interaction parameter items measuring referent units partitioning iterating attributes. title: Pathways for instructionally embedded assessment (PIE) — pie_ft_data
text: 5.RA..1b grade 5 mathematics standard Relationships Algebraic Thinking (RA) domain. Specifically, standard comes Cluster (Represent analyze patterns relationships), represents Expectation 1 (Investigate relationship two numeric patterns), sub-expectation B (Translate two numeric patterns two sets ordered pairs). learning pathway developed 5.RA..1b includes three vertical levels build toward intended learning target. Level 1 (L1) includes emerging concepts skills, Level 2 (L2) includes skills approaching learning target, Level 3 (L3) represents learning target directly aligned learning standard. learning pathway, levels : Level 1: Recognize order elements repeating pattern. Level 2: Organize two numeric patterns table. Level 3: Translate two numeric patterns ordered pairs. additional information development learning pathways, see Kim et al. (2024).

title: Pathways for instructionally embedded assessment (PIE) — pie_ft_data
text: PIE field test administered fixed-form assessment Spring 2024. field test, student assessed three levels within learning pathway single point time. Following field test, test development team reviewed item data selected items promotion pilot item pool. pie_ft_data includes items promoted use pilot administration. information PIE field test, see ATLAS (2025a). pilot administration, teachers chose assess learning standards. PIE assessments intended administered three points instructional cycle. first assessment (baseline) administered instructional cycle. baseline assessment measured Level 1 skills, allowing teachers evaluate whether students ready instruction standard. instruction occurred, teachers administered second assessment (midway). midway assessment measured Level 2 skills students additionally re-assessed Level 1 skills students demonstrate mastery skills baseline assessment. Teachers can observe students , evaluate progress made, plan additional targeted instruction. final assessment administered end instructional unit (end_of_unit). assessment included items measuring Level 3 skills students, well Level 2 skills students demonstrate mastery skills midway assessment. Teachers use information monitor students' progress make decisions next instructional steps. total, three pathway levels assessed three timepoints. additional information pilot design administration, see ATLAS (2025b). Notably, one--one correspondence items included field test pilot data sets. three items promoted following field test, administered pilot. items either held back event items included pilot pool removed replaced pilot administration, assigned fixed-form end--year assessment included data. Additionally, five items administered pilot directly field tested. items "twins" items field tested. details item twinning approach, see ATLAS (2025a).

title: Rapid online assessment of reading and phonological awareness (ROAR-PA) — roarpa_data
Rapid online assessment of reading and phonological awareness (ROAR-PA) — roarpa_data
text: roarpa_data tibble containing response data 272 rows 58 variables: id: Respondent identifier. del_10-lsm_05: Dichotomous item responses 57 ROAR-PA items. roarpa_qmatrix tibble identifies ROAR-PA subtest measured item. data set contains 57 items measuring 3 attributes. roarpa_qmatrix therefore 57 rows 4 variables: item: Item identifier, corresponds item response columns roarpa_data. Attribute columns: 3 columns, one attribute. dichotomous indicator whether attribute measured item. values 1 indicates attribute measured item value 0 indicates attribute measured item.

title: Rapid online assessment of reading and phonological awareness (ROAR-PA) — roarpa_data
text: ROAR-PA assessment consists 5 subtests, three included data set: fsm: First sound matching lsm: Last sound matching del: Deletion

title: Rapid online assessment of reading and phonological awareness (ROAR-PA) — roarpa_data
text: Domingue, B., Braginsky, M., Caffrey-Maffei, L., Gilbert, J. B., Kanopka, K., Kapoor, R., Lee, H., Liu, Y., Nadela, S., Pan, G., Zhang, L., Zhang, S., & Frank, M. C. (2025). introduction Item Response Warehouse (IRW): resource enhancing data usage psychometrics. Behavior Research Methods, 57, Article 276. doi:10.3758/s13428-025-02796-y Gijbels, L., Burkhardt, ., Ma, W. ., & Yeatman, J. D. (2024). Rapid online assessment reading phonological awareness (ROAR-PA). Scientific Reports, 14, Article 10249. doi:10.1038/s41598-024-60834-9

what: Trends in international mathematics and science study (TIMSS) assessment for grade 8 mathematics (2003) — timss03_data
title: Trends in international mathematics and science study (TIMSS) assessment for grade 8 mathematics (2003) — timss03_data
text: data United States sample 2003 TIMSS assessment grade 8 mathematics. data contains responses 23 items 757 respondents. data used Skaggs et al. (2016) Su et al. (2013) evaluate appropriateness using diagnostic models modeling TIMSS assessment data. data set uploaded Item Response Warehouse (Domingue et al., 2025) reformatted .

title: Trends in international mathematics and science study (TIMSS) assessment for grade 8 mathematics (2003) — timss03_data
Trends in international mathematics and science study (TIMSS) assessment for grade 8 mathematics (2003) — timss03_data
text: timss03_data tibble containing TIMSS response data 757 rows 24 variables. id: Respondent identifier. M012001-M022234B: Dichotomous item responses 23 TIMSS items. timss03_qmatrix tibble identifies skills measured TIMSS 2003 item. data set contains subset data consisting 23 items measuring 13 skills. timss03_qmatrix correspondingly made 23 rows 14 variables. item: Item identifier, corresponds item response columns timss03_data. Attribute columns: 13 columns, one attribute. dichotomous indicator whether skill measured item. value 1 indicates skill measured item value 0 indicates skill measured item.

title: Trends in international mathematics and science study (TIMSS) assessment for grade 8 mathematics (2003) — timss03_data
text: skills correspond knowledge : understand_ratio: Understand concepts ratio unit rate. use_ratio: Use ratio rate reasoning solve problems. compute_fluently: Compute fluently multi-digit numbers. rational_numbers: Apply extend understandings numbers system rational numbers. algebraic_expressions: Apply extend understandings arithmetic algebraic expressions. one_variable_equations: Solve one-variable equations inequalities. recognize_proportional_relationships: Recognize represent proportional relationships quantities. use_proportional_relationships: Use proportional relationships solve multi-step ratio percent problems. asmd_rational_numbers: Add, subtract, multiply, divide rational numbers. expressions_equations: Solve problems using numerical algebraic expressions equations. compare_fractions: Compare two fractions different numerators denominators. multistep_problems: Solve multi-step problems whole numbers using four operations. equivalent_fractions: Use equivalent fraction strategy add subtract fractions. details, see Table 2 Su et al. (2013).

title: Trends in international mathematics and science study (TIMSS) assessment for grade 8 mathematics (2003) — timss03_data
text: Domingue, B., Braginsky, M., Caffrey-Maffei, L., Gilbert, J. B., Kanopka, K., Kapoor, R., Lee, H., Liu, Y., Nadela, S., Pan, G., Zhang, L., Zhang, S., & Frank, M. C. (2025). introduction Item Response Warehouse (IRW): resource enhancing data usage psychometrics. Behavior Research Methods, 57, Article 276. doi:10.3758/s13428-025-02796-y Skaggs, G., Wilkins, J. L. M., & Hein, S. F. (2016). Grain size parameter recovery TIMSS general diagnostic model. International Journal Testing, 16(4), 310-330. doi:10.1080/15305058.2016.1145683 Su, Y.-L., Choi, K. M., Lee, W.-C., Choi, T., & McAninch, M. (2013). Hierarchical cognitive diagnostic analysis TIMSS 2003 mathematics (Research Report . 35). University Iowa, Center Advanced Studies Measurement Assessment. https://education.uiowa.edu/sites/education.uiowa.edu/files/2022-10/casma-research-report-35.pdf

what: Trends in international mathematics and science study (TIMSS) assessment for grade 4 mathematics (2007) — timss07_data
title: Trends in international mathematics and science study (TIMSS) assessment for grade 4 mathematics (2007) — timss07_data
text: data United States sample 2007 TIMSS assessment grade 4 mathematics. data contains responses 25 items 698 respondents used previously Lee et al. (2011) Park et al. (2014, 2018) estimate diagnostic classification models. data set uploaded Item Response Warehouse (Domingue et al., 2025) reformatted .

title: Trends in international mathematics and science study (TIMSS) assessment for grade 4 mathematics (2007) — timss07_data
Trends in international mathematics and science study (TIMSS) assessment for grade 4 mathematics (2007) — timss07_data
text: timss07_data tibble containing TIMSS response data 698 rows 26 variables. id: Respondent identifier. M031085-M041258B: Dichotomous item responses 25 TIMSS items. timss07_skill_qmatrix tibble identifies skills measured TIMSS 2007 item. timss07_skill_qmatrix made 25 rows 16 variables. item: Item identifier, corresponds item response columns timss07_data. Attribute columns: 15 columns, one attribute. Attributes named {domain}_{topic}_{skill}. example n_wn_represent skill "Representing, comparing, ordering whole numbers well demonstrating knowledge place value," falls "Whole Numbers" topic (wn) "Number" domain (n). See Details complete list skills. column dichotomous indicator whether skill measured item. value 1 indicates skill measured item value 0 indicates skill measured item. timss07_topic_qmatrix tibble identifies topics measured TIMSS 2007 item. form Q-matrix used Park et al. (2014, 2018), combined "Number Sentences Whole Numbers" "Patterns Relationships" topics "Number" domain single attributes (n_nspr), well "Reading Interpreting" "Organizing Representing" topics "Data & Display" domain (dd_rior). Thus, timss07_topic_qmatrix made 25 rows 8 variables. item: Item identifier, corresponds item response columns timss07_data. Attribute columns: 7 columns, one attribute. Attributes named {domain}_{topic}. example gm_lm topic "Location Movement," falls "Geometric Shapes & Measurement" (gm) domain. column dichotomous indicator whether topic measured item. value 1 indicates topic measured item value 0 indicates topic measured item. See Details complete list topics. timss07_domain_qmatrix tibble identifies domains measured TIMSS 2007 item. timss07_domain_qmatrix made 25 rows 3 variables. item: Item identifier, corresponds item response columns timss07_data. Attribute columns: 3 columns, one attribute. Attributes named {domain}. example dd domain "Data & Display." column dichotomous indicator whether domain measured item. value 1 indicates domain measured item value 0 indicates domain measured item. See Details complete list domains.

title: Trends in international mathematics and science study (TIMSS) assessment for grade 4 mathematics (2007) — timss07_data
text: skills 2007 TIMSS organized domains topics. Attribute names Q-matrices named combining hierarchical elements. example, timss07_skill_qmatrix attributes names {domain}_{topic}_{skill}, whereas attributes timss07_topic_qmatrix named {domain}_{topic}. details, see Table 2 Lee et al. (2011).

title: Trends in international mathematics and science study (TIMSS) assessment for grade 4 mathematics (2007) — timss07_data
text: Domingue, B., Braginsky, M., Caffrey-Maffei, L., Gilbert, J. B., Kanopka, K., Kapoor, R., Lee, H., Liu, Y., Nadela, S., Pan, G., Zhang, L., Zhang, S., & Frank, M. C. (2025). introduction Item Response Warehouse (IRW): resource enhancing data usage psychometrics. Behavior Research Methods, 57, Article 276. doi:10.3758/s13428-025-02796-y Lee, Y.-S., Park, Y. S., & Taylan, D. (2011). cognitive diagnostic modeling attribute mastery Massachusetts, Minnesota, U.S. national sample using TIMSS 2007. International Journal Testing, 11(2), 144-177. doi:10.1080/15305058.2010.534571 Park, Y. S., & Lee, Y.-S. (2014). extension DINA model using covariates: Examining factors affecting response probability latent classification. Applied Psychological Measurement, 38(5), 376-390. doi:10.1177/0146621614523830 Park, Y. S., Xing, K., & Lee, Y.-S. (2018). Explanatory cognitive diagnostic models: Incorporating latent observed predictors. Applied Psychological Measurement, 42(5), 376-392. doi:10.1177/0146621617738012

title: dcmdata (development version)
text: Added new data Pathways Instructionally Embedded Assessment (PIE) project: pie_ft_data, pie_ft_qmatrix, pie_pilot_data, pie_pilot_qmatrix. Added new data sets Item Response Warehouse. Fraction subtraction: fraction_data, fraction_qmatrix. MCMI-III: mcmi_data, mcmi_qmatrix. ROAR-PA: roarpa_data, roarpa_qmatrix. TIMSS (2003): timss03_data, timss03_qmatrix. TIMSS (2007): timss07_data, timss07_skill_qmatrix, timss07_topic_qmatrix, timss07_domain_qmatrix. Added NEWS.md file track changes package.
